message:
test(harness): add resize storm e2e test for coalescer robustness

Adds a 1000+ line e2e test that subjects the ResizeCoalescer to
extreme resize event patterns: rapid jitter between sizes, monotonic
growth/shrink sequences, and mixed random patterns. Validates that
the coalescer correctly transitions between regimes (calm/burst/storm),
applies resizes within latency bounds, and never drops below minimum
frame pacing.

Also syncs beads issue state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Conformal SLA Monitor (Quantile Bounds)
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

- Guarantees: prefer anytime-valid testing (e-process) or distribution-free calibration (conformal) where applicable

Implement adaptive resize coalescing: latest-wins with bounded latency and regime-aware behavior.

## Scope
- Coalesce events during burst regimes.
- Preserve responsiveness during steady regimes.
- Emit decision logs for each coalesce window.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: bounded latency and correct final size.
- PTY E2E: resize storm scripts with JSONL logs (timestamps, sizes, decisions).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Coalescer never drops the final size.
- Latency bounds respected under burst conditions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:23.063667937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:22:39.942639242Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:22:39.942611660Z","close_reason":"Implemented ResizeCoalescer with latest-wins semantics, regime-aware behavior (steady/burst), hard deadline guarantees, event rate tracking, decision logging, and 16 unit tests.  {"id":"bd-agng","title":"Implement recovery mechanism for budget upgrade","description":"# Implement recovery mechanism for budget upgrade

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). When load decreases, the system should recover to higher fidelity. This must be gradual and stable.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Budget recovery tracker
pub struct BudgetRecovery {
    /// Current degradation level
    current_level: DegradationLevel,
    /// Frames at current level under budget
    stable_frames: u32,
    /// Required stable frames before upgrade
    threshold: u32,
    /// Frames since last degradation
    frames_since_degrade: u32,
    /// Minimum frames before allowing upgrade after degradation
    min_recovery_delay: u32,
}

impl BudgetRecovery {
    pub fn new(threshold: u32) -> Self {
        Self {
            current_level: DegradationLevel::Full,
            stable_frames: 0,
            threshold,
            frames_since_degrade: u32::MAX / 2,  // Start as if long stable
            min_recovery_delay: 10,
        }
    }
    
    /// Record a frame that finished under budget
    pub fn record_under_budget(&mut self) {
        self.stable_frames = self.stable_frames.saturating_add(1);
        self.frames_since_degrade = self.frames_since_degrade.saturating_add(1);
    }
    
    /// Record a frame that exceeded budget
    pub fn record_over_budget(&mut self) {
        self.stable_frames = 0;
    }
    
    /// Check if we should upgrade to higher fidelity
    pub fn should_upgrade(&self) -> bool {
        self.current_level != DegradationLevel::Full
            && self.stable_frames >= self.threshold
            && self.frames_since_degrade >= self.min_recovery_delay
    }
    
    /// Perform upgrade
    pub fn upgrade(&mut self) {
        if self.current_level != DegradationLevel::Full {
            let old = self.current_level;
            self.current_level = self.current_level.upgrade();
            self.stable_frames = 0;  // Reset stability counter
            
            tracing::info!(
                from = ?old,
                to = ?self.current_level,
                "Upgrading render fidelity after stable performance"
            );
        }
    }
    
    /// Record degradation (resets recovery progress)
    pub fn on_degrade(&mut self, new_level: DegradationLevel) {
        self.current_level = new_level;
        self.stable_frames = 0;
        self.frames_since_degrade = 0;
    }
}
```

## Why Recovery Delay?
- Prevents ping-pong between levels
- Allows system to stabilize after load spike
- Users see gradual improvement, not flickering

## Test Requirements
- [ ] stable_frames increments on under_budget
- [ ] stable_frames resets on over_budget
- [ ] should_upgrade requires threshold AND delay
- [ ] upgrade() advances level and logs
- [ ] on_degrade() resets all counters

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BudgetRecovery struct implemented
- [ ] Gradual upgrade (one level at a time)
- [ ] Stability threshold enforced
- [ ] Recovery delay prevents oscillation
- [ ] Logging on upgrade events","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:21.960433851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:18:41.208616362Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:18:41.208597797Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-1iqn in budget.rs - should_upgrade(), upgrade(), cooldown tracking in RenderBudget struct","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-agng","depends_on_id":"bd-3tfu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-atwq","title":"Text Effects: Chromatic Aberration / RGB Split","description":"## Background
Chromatic aberration (RGB channel separation) is a popular effect in modern UI design, creating a '3D glasses' or glitch aesthetic. High visual impact for low implementation effort.

## Implementation

Add TextEffect variant:
```rust
TextEffect::ChromaticAberration {
    offset: u8,           // Cell offset 1-5 (typically 1-2)
    direction: Direction, // Which way red/blue shift
    animated: bool,       // Oscillate offset over time
    speed: f64,           // Oscillations per second if animated
}
```

### Rendering Approach (Terminal-Friendly):

Since terminals use character cells (not pixels), we simulate chromatic aberration by color-shifting based on position:

```rust
fn chromatic_color(&self, base_color: PackedRgba, idx: usize, total: usize) -> PackedRgba {
    let offset = self.offset as f64;
    let center = total as f64 / 2.0;
    let distance_from_center = (idx as f64 - center) / center;  // -1 to 1
    
    // Left side: boost red, reduce blue
    // Right side: boost blue, reduce red
    let red_boost = (-distance_from_center * offset * 30.0).clamp(-50.0, 50.0);
    let blue_boost = (distance_from_center * offset * 30.0).clamp(-50.0, 50.0);
    
    PackedRgba::rgb(
        (base_color.r() as i16 + red_boost as i16).clamp(0, 255) as u8,
        base_color.g(),
        (base_color.b() as i16 + blue_boost as i16).clamp(0, 255) as u8,
    )
}
```

### Direction Options:
- Horizontal (default): Red left, Blue right
- Vertical: Red top, Blue bottom

### Animation:
When animated=true, offset oscillates: actual_offset = offset * sin(time * speed * 2π)

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_chromatic_offset_0_normal: No color shift at offset 0
- test_chromatic_left_chars_redder: Left side has more red
- test_chromatic_right_chars_bluer: Right side has more blue
- test_chromatic_center_unchanged: Center char minimal shift
- test_chromatic_animated_varies: Different time = different shift
- test_chromatic_offset_clamped: Colors stay in 0-255 range
- test_chromatic_direction_vertical: Top/bottom shift instead

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot 'ABERRATION' with offset=2
- Snapshot same text at t=0 vs t=0.5 with animation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Chromatic Aberration / RGB Split
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-b8m5","title":"Define CapturedError struct for panic information","description":"# Define CapturedError struct for panic information

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). When a widget panics, we need to capture comprehensive diagnostic information for debugging.

## Technical Design

```rust
use std::any::Any;
use std::backtrace::Backtrace;
use std::time::Instant;

/// Captured error from a widget panic
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct CapturedError {
    /// Error message extracted from panic
    pub message: String,
    
    /// Name of the widget that panicked
    pub widget_name: &'static str,
    
    /// Backtrace at panic point (if available)
    pub backtrace: Option<String>,
    
    /// When the panic occurred
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    
    /// Area the widget was rendering when it panicked
    pub area: Rect,
    
    /// Render phase when panic occurred
    pub phase: RenderPhase,
    
    /// Parent widget chain (for context)
    pub widget_path: Vec<&'static str>,
}

/// Phase of rendering when error occurred
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum RenderPhase {
    /// During layout/constraint solving
    Layout,
    /// During actual buffer writes
    Render,
    /// During event handling
    Update,
}

impl CapturedError {
    /// Create from a caught panic
    pub fn from_panic(
        panic_info: Box<dyn Any + Send>,
        widget_name: &'static str,
        area: Rect,
        phase: RenderPhase,
    ) -> Self {
        // Extract message from panic payload
        let message = if let Some(s) = panic_info.downcast_ref::<&str>() {
            s.to_string()
        } else if let Some(s) = panic_info.downcast_ref::<String>() {
            s.clone()
        } else {
            "Unknown panic".to_string()
        };
        
        // Capture backtrace
        let backtrace = match std::backtrace::Backtrace::capture().status() {
            std::backtrace::BacktraceStatus::Captured => {
                Some(std::backtrace::Backtrace::capture().to_string())
            }
            _ => None,
        };
        
        Self {
            message,
            widget_name,
            backtrace,
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            area,
            phase,
            widget_path: Vec::new(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Add parent widget to path (for nested boundaries)
    pub fn with_parent(mut self, parent: &'static str) -> Self {
        self.widget_path.push(parent);
        self
    }
    
    /// Format for logging
    pub fn log_format(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "Widget panic in {}: {} at {:?}",
            self.widget_name,
            self.message,
            self.area
        )
    }
    
    /// Format for user display (truncated)
    pub fn display_format(&self, max_len: usize) -> String {
        let msg: String = self.message.chars().take(max_len.saturating_sub(4)).collect();
        format!("[!] {}...", msg)
    }
}
```

## Why This Design
1. **Rich context**: Widget name, area, phase all captured
2. **Backtrace optional**: May not be available in all builds
3. **Widget path**: Shows nesting for complex UIs
4. **Two formats**: Full for logs, truncated for display

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CapturedError struct with all fields
- [ ] from_panic extracts message from Any
- [ ] Backtrace captured when available
- [ ] log_format() for tracing
- [ ] display_format() for UI","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:16.386273542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:09.284105824Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:09.284072601Z","close_reason":"CapturedError struct already implemented as part of bd-1euu in ftui-widgets/src/error_boundary.rs with message, widget_name, area, timestamp fields and from_panic constructor","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b8m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1euu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-bksf","title":"Demo Showcase: Mouse/Hit-Test Playground","description":"## Background

Mouse support in terminals is notoriously inconsistent (modes, encodings, multiplexers). FrankenTUI supports rich input parsing, and this screen exists to:
- prove correctness of mouse event decoding (SGR, scroll, drag)
- prove correctness of hit-testing and hover state
- provide a playground to tune jitter/hysteresis policies

It should feel like a "mouse lab" with immediate visual feedback.

## UX / Core Interactions

- A canvas of interactive widgets:
  - buttons, scrollbars, draggable sliders
  - a small grid of hit-test targets to test edge boundaries
- Live event readout:
  - last N mouse events (type, coords, modifiers)
  - current hover target ID
- Optional overlay:
  - draw hit-test boxes and hovered region outline

Suggested keybindings:
- O: toggle hit-test overlay
- J: toggle jitter stabilization visualization
- C: clear event log

## Architecture Notes

- Drive events from ftui-core mouse parsing.
- Hit-test index + dirty-rect cache are validated by bd-2qag.
- Hover jitter stabilization policy validated by bd-9n09.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Demonstrates all major mouse event classes:
  - down/up for left/middle/right
  - move
  - drag
  - scroll up/down
- Hover state is stable (no flicker) when the pointer micro-jitters.
- Overlay rendering is deterministic.

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- Jitter stabilization (bd-9n09):
  - model hover transitions as change-point detection with hysteresis
  - log decisions with a tiny evidence ledger (why hover changed or stayed)

## Extreme Optimization

- Hit-testing must be near O(1) on average with bounded allocations.
- Add a stress case with dense hit regions to validate budgets.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:36.969355921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T16:30:24.146508748Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bksf","depends_on_id":"bd-2qag","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-bksf","depends_on_id":"bd-9n09","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":498,"issue_id":"bd-bksf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Showcase mouse events, hover effects, and scrollbars.
- Demonstrate hit-grid accuracy.

Testing
- Unit tests for hit-test mapping.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs for mouse event handling.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:37Z"}]}
